items.headline: भारत की मेघना सज्जनार ने एशियन शूटिंग चैंपियनशिप में जीता ब्रॉन्ज़ मेडल
whyItMatters: अब इस लाइव ब्लॉग को रोकने का वक़्त आ गया है. बीबीसी संवाददाता चंदन कुमार जजवाड़े को दीजिए इजाज़त. कल एक नए लाइव ब्लॉग के साथ फिर हाज़िर होंगे. बीबीसी हिन्दी पर मौजूद कुछ अहम ख़बरों को पढ़ने के लिए आप
summaryParagraphs: अब इस लाइव ब्लॉग को रोकने का वक़्त आ गया है. बीबीसी संवाददाता चंदन कुमार जजवाड़े को दीजिए इजाज़त. कल एक नए लाइव ब्लॉग के साथ फिर हाज़िर होंगे. बीबीसी हिन्दी पर मौजूद कुछ अहम ख़बरों को पढ़ने के लिए आप उनके साथ लगे लिंक पर क्लिक कर सकते हैं. - रूसी तेल पर पीयूष गोयल ने नहीं दिया कोई जवाब, जानें कौन से अमेरिकी उत्पादों पर टैरिफ़ हटा देगा भारत - पटना में गर्ल्स हॉस्टल के हालात : 'दिल्ली महंगा था, लेकिन यहां तो जान पर ही ख़तरा है' - मध्य प्रदेश: मधुमक्खियों से 25 बच्चों को बचाने के लिए अपनी जान गंवाने वाली कंचन बाई - 'हम सर कटाने को तैयार', राजस्थान में खेजड़ी के पेड़ काटे जाने के ख़िलाफ़ धरना For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. items.headline: American Air CEO Agrees to Meet Pilots Union After Criticism
whyItMatters: (Bloomberg) -- American Airlines Group Inc. Chief Executive Office Robert Isom on Saturday responded to a flood of concerns from pilots about the company’s underperformance, days after the union threa
summaryParagraphs: (Bloomberg) -- American Airlines Group Inc. Chief Executive Office Robert Isom on Saturday responded to a flood of concerns from pilots about the company’s underperformance, days after the union threatened to hold a vote of no confidence in management.  The board of the Allied Pilots Association said in a letter late Friday that it wants Nick Silva, the union’s president, to formally present its concerns to American’s directors, arguing that the airline lacks a clear identity or credible long-term strategy.  “Our airline is on an underperforming path and has failed to define an identity or a strategy to correct course,” the union wrote in the letter. Isom on Saturday responded in his own letter saying he’s “interested in addressing the issues that most concern our pilots,” including the handling of winter storms, attendance policies and the business plan to “return American to its rightful place atop the industry.”  Pilots have blamed leadership for what they described a For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

items.headline: 4 Indian students injured in knife attack at Russian medical college hostel
whyItMatters: UPDATED: Feb 8, 2026 00:15 IST Several persons, including four Indian students, were injured in a stabbing attack on Saturday at a sports hall inside a foreign students’ hostel at a university i
summaryParagraphs: UPDATED: Feb 8, 2026 00:15 IST Several persons, including four Indian students, were injured in a stabbing attack on Saturday at a sports hall inside a foreign students’ hostel at a university in Russia’s Bashkortostan Republic, local media reported. Two police officers were also injured while attempting to intervene, according to preliminary information. The incident occurred at the dormitory of the State Medical University in Ufa, the capital city of Bashkortostan. The Interior Ministry said initial reports indicate that a teenager armed with a knife entered the medical university dormitory and attacked students residing there, stabbing several individuals. “The attacker resisted arrest, during which two police officers were stabbed. Furthermore, the suspect also inflicted bodily harm on himself,” Interior Ministry spokesperson Maj Gen Irina Volk told the RTVI.com web portal. Authorities said the suspect was detained following the attack. The extent of injurie For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

items.headline: Islamic State affiliate claims Islamabad mosque attack that killed over 50
whyItMatters: New Delhi , UPDATED: Feb 7, 2026 23:31 IST Written By: Mayukh Debnath An affiliate of the terrorist group Islamic State has claimed responsibility for the suicide bombing that claimed the lives of at
summaryParagraphs: New Delhi , UPDATED: Feb 7, 2026 23:31 IST Written By: Mayukh Debnath An affiliate of the terrorist group Islamic State has claimed responsibility for the suicide bombing that claimed the lives of at least 50 people at a Shi'ite mosque in Pakistan's Islamabad on Friday. This came as four suspects, including an alleged mastermind, were arrested in connection with the attack. The explosion that took place at the Khadija Tul Kubra Imambargah also left more than 100 people injured . Pakistani authorities have identified the suicide bomber as 32-year-old Yasir, who hailed from Peshawar in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. While claiming responsibility for the blast in a statement released via its Amaq News Agency, the Islamic State in Pakistan confirmed that the security team at the site tried to stop its bomber . The perpetrator, subsequently, opened fire at the security guards and exploded near the worshippers inside the mosque, according to the terrorist group's communique. It justified the attack, as For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Web.

whyItMatters: A three-storey building housing a restaurant collapsed in Kota, Rajasthan, killing at least two people and leaving several others feared trapped. Rescue operations are underway, with five individuals
summaryParagraphs: A three-storey building housing a restaurant collapsed in Kota, Rajasthan, killing at least two people and leaving several others feared trapped. Rescue operations are underway, with five individuals already rescued and hospitalized. The incident occurred in the Talwandi area, and authorities are working to ascertain the full extent of casualties. For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Times of India.

items.headline: 'Khejri Bachao Andolan': Why Rajasthan is rallying to protect its 'Kalpavriksha'
whyItMatters: A significant protest is under way in Rajasthan over the felling of the khejri tree. Communities are demanding stronger protection for the state tree, alleging that it is being cut to make way for sol
summaryParagraphs: A significant protest is under way in Rajasthan over the felling of the khejri tree. Communities are demanding stronger protection for the state tree, alleging that it is being cut to make way for solar power projects. Political leaders across parties have joined the movement, drawing attention to the tree’s cultural and ecological significance. In an arid landscape where survival is closely tied to ecology, the debate in western Rajasthan now extends beyond development to questions of history, identity and survival. For the full story and latest updates, read the complete article. Source: Times of India.
